rationale: In the theory-development section, the citing paper uses Homburg et al. (2015) as support for a specific mechanism claim: that idea-focused expression entails greater elaboration. That mechanism is used to motivate why idea sharing should increase engagement relative to identity sharing, so the citation contributes to conceptual setup rather than serving as a generic literature-list mention. At the same time, the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's sentiment-measurement method or empirical design, so the role is conceptual support instead of direct extension or application.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Homburg et al. (2015) to justify its mechanism that sharing ideas requires greater elaboration than sharing identity content. This support helps frame the proposed "idea advantage" in online learner engagement.
